name: mx-kv
description: Local file-backed key-value store for 八百万重工 agents (string, counter, list, history, and state types).

env_vars.MX_CURRENT_AGENT: Selects which agent's per-agent KV namespace is read or written.
env_vars.MX_HOME: Root of the mx data directory; the KV schema and data files live underneath it.

pathways.name: check-state
pathways.description: Discover which keys exist and read a current value.

steps.note: List every defined key with its type.
Read the current value of a string/counter/state key.

pathways.name: track-history
pathways.description: Append an entry to a history/list key and read the most recent entries back.

steps.note: Append a timestamped entry (use --create history to auto-create the key).
Read the last N entries to confirm the append.

pathways.name: adjust-counter
pathways.description: Read a counter, increment it, and read it back.

steps.note: Increment the counter (use --by N for a larger step).

errors.description: The requested key, entry index, or hash does not exist.
errors.resolution: Run 'mx kv keys' to list valid keys, or push an entry with --create to auto-create the key.

errors.description: The operation is not valid for the key's type (e.g. 'inc' on a string, 'pop' on a history).
errors.resolution: Check the key's type with 'mx kv keys' and use a read/write verb that matches it.

errors.description: The key exists in the schema but has no data yet.
errors.resolution: Set or push a value before reading.

keys.description: List all defined keys and their types.
keys.agent_description: Enumerate every KV key with its type before reading or writing — the cheapest way to orient.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human table.

examples.description: List all keys as JSON for parsing.
examples.note: Always run this first when you do not already know a key's type.

get.description: Read the current value of a key.
get.agent_description: Read a string/counter/state value, or a specific list/history entry via --id.

args.name: key
args.description: Key name to read.

flags.name: --id
flags.description: Entry index, range (35-64), or comma list (1,5,12) for list/history keys.

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Resolve a linked kn- memory reference into its full content.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human value.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Current value (string, integer, JSON object, or array of entries).

examples.description: Read a string key and parse the JSON result.
examples.note: Prefer --json whenever the value will be parsed programmatically.

examples.description: Read a range of history entries by index.
examples.note: Ranges and comma lists only apply to list and history keys.

set.description: Set a string/counter value, or a single field of a state key.
set.agent_description: Write a string or counter value; for state keys pass FIELD then VALUE.

args.name: key
args.description: Key name to write.

args.name: field_or_value
args.description: For string/counter keys this is the value; for state keys this is the field name.

args.name: value
args.description: The value, when setting a state key field (FIELD VALUE form).

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Link a kn- memory entry to this value.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human confirmation.

fields.name: previous
fields.description: The prior value, if the key already existed.

examples.invocation: mx kv set 焦点 "ship the manifest CLI"
examples.note: String and counter keys take a single VALUE argument.

examples.description: Set one field of a state key.
examples.invocation: mx kv set deploy status green
examples.note: State keys take FIELD then VALUE; the key must already be of type state.

args.name: key
args.description: Counter key to increment.

flags.name: --by
flags.description: Step size to add.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human confirmation.

args.name: key
args.description: Counter key to decrement.

flags.name: --by
flags.description: Step size to subtract.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human confirmation.

push.description: Append an entry to a list or history key.
push.agent_description: Append a value (optionally with structured --data) to a list/history; --create makes the key if missing.

args.name: key
args.description: List or history key to append to.

args.name: value
args.description: The entry value.

flags.name: --data
flags.description: Structured JSON data attached to the entry.

flags.name: --memory
flags.description: Link a kn- memory entry to this entry.

flags.name: --create
flags.description: Auto-create the key with this type if it does not exist.

flags.name: --max-entries
flags.description: Cap retained entries when creating the key (used with --create).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human confirmation.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Index assigned to the new entry.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Stable kv- hash of the new entry.

examples.description: Append a finding to a history key, creating it if absent.
examples.invocation: mx kv push 知見 "clippy denies all warnings in CI" --create history
examples.note: Use --create the first time you write a key that is not yet in the schema.

pop.description: Remove and return the last entry of a list key.

args.name: key
args.description: List key to pop from.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human value.

remove.description: Remove entries from a list or history key by index, hash, or matching substring.
remove.agent_description: Delete entries by --id (index or kv- hash) or by substring; add --all to remove every match.

args.name: key
args.description: List or history key to remove from.

args.name: substring
args.description: Substring to match for removal (omit when using --id).

flags.name: --id
flags.description: Entry index or kv- hash to remove.

flags.name: --all
flags.description: Remove every entry matching the substring, not just the first.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human confirmation.

reset.description: Reset a key to its schema default, discarding all current data.
reset.agent_description: Destructive: clears a key back to its schema default. Irreversible — there is no undo.

args.name: key
args.description: Key to reset.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human confirmation.

examples.description: Reset a list key back to empty.
examples.note: Destructive and irreversible — confirm the key name before running.

last.description: Return the most recent entries of a list or history key.

args.name: key
args.description: List or history key to read.

flags.name: --count
flags.description: Number of trailing entries to return.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by a structured data field (key=value); repeatable.

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Only entries since a relative time (e.g. 30m, 1h, 7d, 2w).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human listing.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

since.description: Return entries recorded since a relative time or ISO-8601 timestamp.

args.name: key
args.description: History or list key to read.

args.name: when
args.description: Relative time (30m, 1h, 7d, 2w) or ISO-8601 timestamp.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human listing.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

search.description: Case-insensitive substring search over a list or history key.
search.agent_description: Find entries whose value contains a query string; combine with --where and date filters.

args.name: key
args.description: List or history key to search.

args.name: query
args.description: Substring to match (case-insensitive).

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by a structured data field (key=value); repeatable.

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Only entries since a relative time (e.g. 30m, 1h, 7d, 2w).

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human listing.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

examples.description: Search a history key for entries mentioning a topic.
examples.note: Matching is a plain case-insensitive substring test, not a regex.

count.description: Count entries in a list or history key, optionally matching a substring.

args.name: key
args.description: List or history key to count.

args.name: filter
args.description: Only count entries containing this substring.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by a structured data field (key=value); repeatable.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human count.

random.description: Return random entries from a list or history key.

args.name: key
args.description: List or history key to sample.

flags.name: --count
flags.description: Number of random entries to return.

flags.name: --where
flags.description: Filter by a structured data field (key=value); repeatable.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit JSON instead of the human listing.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was recorded.

dump.description: Dump all KV state as JSON.
dump.agent_description: Read every key and value at once; use --format compact for one line per key.

flags.name: --format
flags.description: Output layout.

fields.name: keys
fields.description: Map of every key to its current value and type.
